content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (post iter-024)

## Lead route: S2 (Hⁱ(C,𝒪_C) finiteness) — clear the HARD GATE, then scaffold
1. **HARD-GATE re-review of the S2 chapter** is mandatory before any prover on `SerreFiniteness.lean`.
   blueprint-reviewer iter-024 left S2 partial/partial; the restructure (drop gate + add
   `lem:HModule_affine_acyclic` + narrow Čech=derived CRUX) was done this iter but is NOT yet re-gated.
   Use the same-iter fast path: after the chapter is confirmed, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer scoped to
   the S2 chapter alone; only a fresh complete+correct verdict clears it.
2. **Scaffold-FIRST, then prove (HAZARD).** S2 creates NEW decls with no pre-existing sorry → a
   `mathlib-build`/prove lane would be auto-dropped by plan-validate ("0 open sorries ⇒ prover quits").
   Dispatch `lean-scaffolder` to land `lem:HModule_affine_acyclic`, `HModule_H1_iso_cech_two_affine`,
   and `cechH1_finiteDimensional` as `sorry` stubs FIRST, THEN a plain prove lane. (Cost two wasted
   rounds on SmoothStalkDVR iter-008/009 — do not repeat.)
3. **Survey the existing Cohomology/* substrate before building (HIGH-VALUE, non-obvious).** A large body
   of Čech/HModule infra is ALREADY landed sorry-free and out-of-cone: `Scheme.HModule'`,
   `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`, `IsCechAcyclicCover`, `cechCochain`/`cechCochain_OC`,
   `module_finite_HModule'_zero_curve`, `subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve`,
   `toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare`, `instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. The S2 "from-scratch" claim is
   true only at the `SerreFiniteness.lean` assembly level. Point the scaffolder/prover at this substrate
   so the Čech↔Ext comparison reuses it rather than rebuilding.
4. **Reversal signal (carried from plan):** if mathlib-analogist finds `HModule_affine_acyclic` needs infra
   deeper than a forgetful-comparison to `Sheaf.H`, effort-break it before scaffolding.

## Coverage debt (1-to-1 tex, per standing "Lean ⇒ tex" doctrine)
Two residual S3-cone helpers are still `unmatched` (no blueprint entry). S3 is closed, so this is
low-priority hygiene, but it should be pinned so the dependency graph stays honest:
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf.carrierSet_zero_eq` — carrier-set unfold helper for the
  zero divisor (WeilDivisor/OcOfD). (Flagged unpinned since iter-020; still open.)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf.coe_eqToHom_of_submodule_eq` — `eqToHom`-on-submodule
  coercion helper.
These are trivial helpers; the planner authors the tex prose (review does not).

## Do NOT do
- Do NOT pin a shared general Grothendieck H^{≥2}=0 gate (incl. RRFormula L469) — it was DROPPED this iter;
  H^{≥2}=0 is free from the 2-element-cover Čech concentration the S2 CRUX already needs.
- Do NOT treat the 144 unmatched substrate nodes (Genus0BaseObjects/*, Cohomology/*, RigidityLemma,
  ProjectiveLineBar/Gm/Ga scaling) as coverage debt — standing policy: out-of-cone, no blueprint owed.
- Do NOT re-open OcOfD for the off-cone `sheafOf_singlePoint_iso` (L772, dag used-by 0) unless a real
  consumer appears.

## Carried (low priority)
- OcOfD.lean stale status docstrings (module header, "3-tier disclosure", L647–668, cokernel/B1 comments)
  — comment-only, fold into any future OcOfD prover lane. Review cannot edit `.lean`.
